board_csa_architect.notes: Uses existing schema columns and the established tempToken pre-auth pattern from MFA flow. No new tables. No scalability or North Star concerns.

board_ciso_cybersecurity.notes: tempToken role-scoped to pre-auth-password-rotation only. Current password verification required. Audit trail via auth_audit_log. Anti-AC covers same-password attacks and token reuse.

board_caio_fullstack.notes: No AI inference, no VRAM budget impact, no prompt changes. Clean auth endpoint addition following existing bcrypt/JWT patterns in auth.ts.

board_vp_ops_devops.notes: 3 AM test passes. Endpoint is stateless, no external dependencies. Uses existing Postgres users table and Redis for rate limiting. No deployment or monitoring changes required.

board_red_team.notes: Conditions: 1) Rate limiter MUST apply to rotate-password endpoint (max 5 attempts per tempToken). 2) Password strength validation MUST be enforced on new password. 3) tempToken MUST be invalidated after successful rotation. All 3 are already implied by spec Anti-AC but must be explicitly tested. STRIDE: all 6 categories covered — tempToken scoping, bcrypt verification, audit trail, generic errors, rate limiting, role isolation.
